title: senml: field_count uses bool array which may not optimize well
description:
File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Lines: 130-144 (field_count function)

The field_count function creates an array of 8 booleans and filters/counts them:
```rust
fn field_count(r: &Record<'_>) -> u64 {
    [
        r.base_name.is_some(),
        // ...8 fields...
    ]
    .iter()
    .filter(|&&x| x)
    .count() as u64
}
```

This is elegant code but may not optimize as well as direct counting for no_std embedded targets:
1. Creates a stack array
2. Iterates with closure allocations (though likely inlined)
3. The closure capture `&&x` is unusual pattern

A simpler version that's guaranteed to compile to efficient code:
```rust
fn field_count(r: &Record<'_>) -> u64 {
    r.base_name.is_some() as u64
        + r.base_time.is_some() as u64
        + r.name.is_some() as u64
        + r.unit.is_some() as u64
        + r.value.is_some() as u64
        + r.string_value.is_some() as u64
        + r.bool_value.is_some() as u64
        + r.time.is_some() as u64
}
```

Impact: Minor performance/code size concern on embedded targets.

Note: The compiler likely optimizes this well; benchmark before changing.
